channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How a CEO and Trump donor is weaponizing tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.
item.content:encoded:
Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.

(Image credit: Richard Tsong-Taatarii/Star Tribune via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Economist Mark Blyth analyses the economy and path ahead for new Federal Reserve chair
item.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks with Brown University economist Mark Blyth about the challenges facing Kevin Warsh, the new chair of the Federal Reserve.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks with Brown University economist Mark Blyth about the challenges facing Kevin Warsh, the new chair of the Federal Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Gen Z homeowners? Yes, more in their 20s are managing to buy despite the odds
item.description: Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.
item.content:encoded:
Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.

(Image credit: Caleb Alvarado for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve
item.description: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.
item.content:encoded:
Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Inflation is up. Americans explain how it's affecting them
item.description: Consumer prices surged 3.8% in April from a year ago, according to the Labor Department. We hear from people who are feeling the effects of inflation and how they're dealing.
item.content:encoded: Consumer prices surged 3.8% in April from a year ago, according to the Labor Department. We hear from people who are feeling the effects of inflation and how they're dealing.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago CEO on inflation and latest consumer price index
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Austan Goolsbee, president and CEO of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago, about rising inflation and the latest federal report on consumer prices.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks Austan Goolsbee, president and CEO of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago, about rising inflation and the latest federal report on consumer prices.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
item.description: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.
item.content:encoded:
Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April. Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis CEO on consumer prices and inflation
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Neel Kashkari, president and CEO of the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis, about consumer prices and primary inflation factors.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Neel Kashkari, president and CEO of the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis, about consumer prices and primary inflation factors.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: The economic chilling effect of Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.
item.content:encoded:
New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: It's not shameful, it's savvy: The shoppers redefining how to save money on groceries
item.description: Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.
item.content:encoded:
Many facing economic pressures and frustrations have begun shopping at budget grocery stores and warehouse clubs in lieu of traditional supermarkets, with priorities shifting in pursuit of good deals.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez
